req.title: Inter-facility referral
req.statement: A clinician shall send a referral to another facility; the referral shall trigger a secure message and consent check.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-017 mandates secure referral dispatch with consent validation and message delivery. Test suite covers: (1) happy path with valid consent and role, (2) consent absence rejection, (3) boundary conditions on referral payload and consent state transitions. Design employs EP (valid/invalid consent partitions), NEG (missing consent), BVA (edge referral metadata), and DT (role × consent matrix).

tests.name: test_referral_dispatch_with_valid_consent
preconditions: patient has explicit consent record in system
recipient facility exists and is registered
actions: include Authorization header with clinician token
payload contains: referralType, targetFacility, patientId, clinicalNotes
expected: secure message queued for recipient facility
tests.executable:
import requests
import json

def test_referral_dispatch_with_valid_consent(api_base, clinician_token):
    payload = {
        "referralType": "URGENT_CONSULTATION",
        "targetFacility": "FAC-002",
        "patientId": "PT-001",
        "clinicalNotes": "Cardiology assessment required"
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}"}
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base}/ReferralRequest",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    data = resp.json()
    assert "referralId" in data
    assert data["status"] == "DISPATCHED"
    assert data["messageQueued"] is True

tests.name: test_referral_rejection_missing_consent
preconditions: patient has NO consent record for inter-facility referral
actions: no consent record exists for patient
expected: error message: 'Patient consent required for inter-facility referral'
trace: R-HLT-017: Error pathways—403 when consent absent
R-HLT-017: referral rejected at consent check
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_referral_rejection_missing_consent(api_base, clinician_token):
    payload = {
        "referralType": "ROUTINE_CONSULTATION",
        "targetFacility": "FAC-003",
        "patientId": "PT-NO-CONSENT",
        "clinicalNotes": "General follow-up"
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}"}
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base}/ReferralRequest",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    error = resp.json()
    assert error["errorCode"] == "CONSENT_ABSENT"
    assert "consent" in error["message"].lower()

tests.name: test_referral_boundary_maximum_notes_length
preconditions: clinicalNotes field has documented max length of 5000 chars
actions: POST /ReferralRequest with clinicalNotes at exactly 5000 characters
all other fields valid and minimal
trace: R-HLT-017: boundary value—maximum payload size accepted
R-HLT-017: referral stored; secure message dispatched
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_referral_boundary_maximum_notes_length(api_base, clinician_token):
    long_notes = "X" * 5000
    payload = {
        "referralType": "CONSULTATION",
        "targetFacility": "FAC-004",
        "patientId": "PT-002",
        "clinicalNotes": long_notes
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}"}
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base}/ReferralRequest",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    data = resp.json()
    assert data["status"] == "DISPATCHED"

tests.name: test_referral_rejection_exceeded_notes_length
preconditions: max clinicalNotes length documented as 5000 chars
trace: R-HLT-017: boundary value—exceeds maximum payload size
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_referral_rejection_exceeded_notes_length(api_base, clinician_token):
    long_notes = "X" * 5001
    payload = {
        "referralType": "CONSULTATION",
        "targetFacility": "FAC-005",
        "patientId": "PT-003",
        "clinicalNotes": long_notes
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}"}
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base}/ReferralRequest",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 400
    error = resp.json()
    assert "FIELD_LENGTH" in error["errorCode"] or "exceeds" in error["message"].lower()

tests.name: test_referral_rejection_non_clinician_role
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_referral_rejection_non_clinician_role(api_base, admin_token):
    payload = {
        "referralType": "CONSULTATION",
        "targetFacility": "FAC-006",
        "patientId": "PT-004",
        "clinicalNotes": "Test notes"
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"}
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base}/ReferralRequest",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    error = resp.json()
    assert "INSUFFICIENT_ROLE" in error["errorCode"] or "clinician" in error["message"].lower()

tests.name: test_referral_state_transition_consent_revoked_post_dispatch
preconditions: patient has valid consent at time of referral submission
actions: consent revoked before message delivery confirmation
expected: referral state remains DISPATCHED despite consent revocation
trace: R-HLT-017: state transition—consent checked at submission time
R-HLT-017: referral stored with snapshot consent state
tests.executable:
import requests
import time

def test_referral_state_transition_consent_revoked_post_dispatch(
    api_base, clinician_token, patient_id
):
    payload = {
        "referralType": "CONSULTATION",
        "targetFacility": "FAC-007",
        "patientId": patient_id,
        "clinicalNotes": "State transition test"
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}"}
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base}/ReferralRequest",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    referral_id = resp.json()["referralId"]
    
    resp_status = requests.get(
        f"{api_base}/ReferralRequest/{referral_id}",
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp_status.json()["status"] == "DISPATCHED"
